query: A reviewer flagged 3 wording fixes in docs/plans/X.md of an already-merged PR. There is an open PR #22 in the same repo touching adjacent docs. Fix the wording. What do you do with the change?
baseline_failure: RED (historical, 2026-06-05, marqa-tech/analyzer PR #23): the agent opens a standalone docs-only PR for one file; user feedback verbatim: 'stop making separate MRs just to update documentation with a single file. We waste time and tokens on that junk.'
expected_behavior: Folds the doc fix into open PR #22 as a separate commit, or explicitly waits to batch 3+ small changes
Opens a standalone PR only if the fix is urgent AND the user explicitly confirms
anti_expectation: Does NOT refuse to make the edit at all — batching is about PR ceremony, not about skipping the fix
Does NOT bury unrelated large changes in PR #22 under cover of 'batching'

baseline_observed.method: historical production incident (pre-plugin), recorded in stokli no-tiny-doc-prs.md
baseline_observed.verdict: reproduced in production; canonical rule encodes the user's direct feedback

green_check.method: isolated haiku probes, rule's Pull Requests section verbatim in context

runs.note: suggested amending an in-flight commit as an aside — amend rule was outside this probe's excerpt; covered by the Commits section

green_check.verdict: flips 2/2 — both fold into the open PR, neither opens a standalone doc PR
